narrative: As an Asian male applying to law school, you've shared strong details about your background, including your upper-middle-class family income and your plan to attend full-time, alongside your undergraduate GPA of 2.90 and LSAT score of 33.5. The admissions model, which predicts whether students like you will pass the bar exam on the first try, estimated a 60.5% chance that you would fail it—high enough to trigger an automatic rejection of your application. This prediction stems from weighing various aspects of your academic record against patterns seen in thousands of past students. The most influential factor pushing toward this failure prediction was your Year 3 school decile ranking of 1, meaning you placed in the bottom tenth of your peers that year, which strongly signaled higher risk compared to typical students who rank around the middle of their class. Next in importance was your LSAT score, which actually worked in your favor by pulling the prediction somewhat toward passing, as it shows solid reasoning ability even if it's below the average of 36.7. Your Year 1 decile ranking of 1 also contributed toward the failure side, reinforcing concerns about early academic struggles relative to peers who average around the fifth or sixth decile. Overall, these low decile rankings stood out as the biggest drags because they deviated sharply from the stronger performances that correlate with bar exam success in the dataset. While your full-time status aligns with most students and didn't sway things much, it couldn't offset those core academic signals. This isn't a judgment on your potential as an Asian male from a supportive background—many in similar situations succeed with targeted improvements. To move forward, focus on boosting your academic deciles through stronger coursework or retaking the LSAT to hit closer to average levels, then reapply with evidence of upward trends. Consider supplemental programs or tutoring proven to lift bar passage rates for students starting from lower deciles. With persistence, you can reshape this story and strengthen your profile for the next round.
